title: Path Traversal
description:
A file download endpoint builds a file path by joining a base directory with a user-supplied filename — but it doesn't verify the result stays within the base directory. An attacker can use `../` sequences to read arbitrary files on the server.

Fix `get_file()` to reject paths that escape the base directory.
hint: Use `os.path.realpath()` on the resolved path and check that it starts with `os.path.realpath(BASE_DIR)`.
explanation: Path traversal (directory traversal) exploits insufficient path validation. `os.path.join` does not prevent `../` escapes. Always resolve the absolute real path and verify it starts with the expected base directory before reading the file.

attack_flow.label: No validation
attack_flow.value: Path escapes /app/files without any boundary check

attack_flow.label: Result
attack_flow.value: /etc/passwd contents returned — sensitive system file exposed

why_blocked: `os.path.realpath()` resolves all `../` segments to an absolute canonical path. Checking that the result starts with the real base directory catches any attempt to escape, regardless of how many `../` sequences are chained.
